etymology.description: 大 (person) + 火 (fire) + 廿 (open mouth screaming) = a person being burned in a sacrificial ceremony -> burnt earth
etymology.interpretation: The character 堇(jǐn, "burnt earth") combines the elements of a person 大(dà, "big") depicted as being part of a sacrifice, with 火(huǒ, "fire") representing a burning action. 廿(niàn, "twenty") is stylized here to depict an open mouth screaming, emphasizing the ritual aspect. The fire ritual thus conveys the idea of burnt earth used in sacrificial ceremonies, believed necessary for invoking rain or fertility in ancient practices.
